title: Announcement on the Inspection of Animal-Origin parts in Import Controls
bodyHtml:
As is known, with the Communiqué Amending the Communiqué on Conformity Inspection of Certain Consumer Products published in the Official Gazette dated 09/04/2025 and numbered 32865, an information requirement has been introduced for consumer products containing animal-origin parts. Accordingly, it has been made mandatory for consumer products containing animal-derived parts to be placed on the market with information indicating from which animal the part was obtained. This information must be provided on the label, packaging, directly on the product, or in an accompanying document. The labeling, which must be done prior to the purchase, is required to be in Turkish, easily visible and legible, and not misleading to consumers.

Within this framework, pursuant to the first paragraph of Article 1 of the Communiqué on Import Inspection of Consumer Products (Product Safety and Inspection: 2025/12) and the Communiqué on Inspection of Certain Textile, Apparel, and Leather Products (Product Safety and Inspection: 2025/18), the aforementioned change will be implemented in import inspections as of 09/06/2025, simultaneously with market surveillance and inspection. Products containing animal-origin parts that lack the required information will not be allowed to be placed on the market. Therefore, if it is determined that the necessary information is missing or incomplete, the products will be handled within the customs area to ensure compliance with the relevant legislation.

Click for the lists of products within the scope:

Communiqué on Import Inspection of Consumer Products (Product Safety and Inspection: 2025/12)

Communiqué on Inspection of Certain Textile, Apparel, and Leather Products (Product Safety and Inspection: 2025/18)
